tasks.title: Add featuredByPlan column, migration, and index update
tasks.description:
Add the new denormalized column accommodations.featured_by_plan boolean NOT NULL DEFAULT false to the Drizzle schema, following the ownerSuspended/planRestricted precedent. Run pnpm db:generate to produce a Drizzle-managed migration (two-carril: structural carril). Update the featured-related indexes (accommodation.dbschema.ts lines 122, 133-135, 142-144): add featuredByPlan to the compound index on isFeatured so the sort on the effective disjunction stays index-assisted. If the composite condition (isFeatured OR featuredByPlan) requires an expression index, write it as an extras carril file (packages/db/src/migrations/extras/) — idempotent, re-applied by pnpm db:apply-extras.

Key files:
- packages/db/src/schemas/accommodation/accommodation.dbschema.ts (add column + update indexes)
- packages/db/src/migrations/XXXX_<slug>.sql (generated by db:generate)
- packages/db/src/migrations/extras/ (optional expression index, if needed)

Acceptance criteria:
- featured_by_plan column exists on the accommodations table, NOT NULL DEFAULT false.
- db:generate produces a clean migration with no other drift.
- Existing isFeatured indexes updated or new indexes cover the effective sort.
- pnpm typecheck green after schema regen.
- Running db:migrate on a fresh DB applies cleanly.

Testing requirements:
- Verify column via Drizzle Studio or a quick psql \d accommodations check.

subtasks.title: Add featuredByPlan boolean column to accommodation.dbschema.ts
Run pnpm db:generate and commit the migration file
Update/add featured indexes to cover isFeatured + featuredByPlan
Write extras file for expression index if required (idempotent)

tasks.title: Update buildAccommodationOrderBy to sort by effective featured disjunction
tasks.description:
Modify buildAccommodationOrderBy in packages/db/src/models/accommodation/accommodation.model.ts (lines 166-196) to order by the effective featured boolean: sql<boolean>`${accommodations.isFeatured} OR ${accommodations.featuredByPlan}` descending, replacing the current desc(accommodations.isFeatured) at line 183-184. The forced featuredFirst: true in apps/api/src/routes/accommodation/public/list.ts:175 is unchanged — that call site still passes featuredFirst and is correct as-is.

Key files:
- packages/db/src/models/accommodation/accommodation.model.ts (buildAccommodationOrderBy)

Acceptance criteria:
- Public listing search returns accommodations with isFeatured=true OR featuredByPlan=true before non-featured ones.
- Neither column alone is sufficient — both independently trigger the prepend.
- pnpm typecheck green.
- The forced featuredFirst path in list.ts is unchanged (no modification to that file).

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-007 search integration test.

subtasks.title: Replace desc(accommodations.isFeatured) with sql OR disjunction in buildAccommodationOrderBy

tasks.title: Remove isFeatured from owner-facing schema and mapper
tasks.description:
Close the owner self-featuring leak. Remove isFeatured (line ~228) from AccommodationUpdateHttpSchema in packages/schemas/src/accommodation/accommodation.http.schema.ts (the schema used at line 362 for owner PATCH). Also remove it from the httpToDomainAccommodationUpdate mapper in the same file or its companion, so the field is stripped before reaching accommodationService.update(). The admin schema (AccommodationPatchInputSchema in accommodation.crud.schema.ts:203) is NOT touched — admins retain manual isFeatured control via PATCH /api/v1/admin/accommodations/:id.

Verify no other owner-facing route or mapper re-introduces isFeatured. The protected PATCH route (apps/api/src/routes/accommodation/protected/patch.ts) uses AccommodationUpdateHttpSchema.partial() — after this change, sending isFeatured in that body should be silently stripped or trigger a 422 (whichever the schema validator produces for unknown fields in this project).

Key files:
- packages/schemas/src/accommodation/accommodation.http.schema.ts (remove isFeatured ~line 228)
- apps/api/src/routes/accommodation/protected/patch.ts (verify it uses the trimmed schema)

Acceptance criteria:
- AccommodationUpdateHttpSchema no longer contains isFeatured.
- httpToDomainAccommodationUpdate does not map isFeatured to the domain update.
- Admin schema (AccommodationPatchInputSchema) is untouched and still accepts isFeatured.
- pnpm typecheck green.
- Covered by T-007 owner-leak regression test.

Testing requirements:
- Covered by T-007: owner PATCH with { isFeatured: true } must not persist the value.

subtasks.title: Remove isFeatured from AccommodationUpdateHttpSchema
Remove isFeatured from httpToDomainAccommodationUpdate mapper
Verify protected patch route uses the trimmed schema

tasks.title: Implement featuredByPlan bulk sync service
tasks.description:
Create a focused sync service that sets or clears featuredByPlan on ALL accommodations belonging to a given owner in a single bulk UPDATE. This is the building block consumed by both the billing event wiring (T-005) and the reconciliation cron (T-006). The service does NOT resolve the entitlement itself — callers pass the resolved active boolean.

Create packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/featured-by-plan.sync.ts:
- Export syncFeaturedByPlan({ ownerId, active }: { ownerId: string; active: boolean }): Promise<{ updated: number }>
- Uses a Drizzle bulk UPDATE: UPDATE accommodations SET featured_by_plan = $active WHERE owner_id = $ownerId AND deleted_at IS NULL
- Logs the result via @repo/logger
- Returns { updated } count for observability
- Follows RO-RO pattern; JSDoc on the exported function

Key files:
- packages/service-core/src/services/accommodation/featured-by-plan.sync.ts (new)
- packages/service-core/src/index.ts or relevant barrel (export the function)

Acceptance criteria:
- syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, true) sets featuredByPlan=true on ALL active (non-deleted) accommodations for that owner.
- syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, false) sets featuredByPlan=false on all.
- Soft-deleted accommodations (deletedAt IS NOT NULL) are excluded from the update.
- Returns accurate { updated } count.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Unit test with mocked Drizzle: set-true, set-false, empty-owner (updated=0), soft-deleted exclusion. Covered by T-007.

subtasks.title: Create featured-by-plan.sync.ts with syncFeaturedByPlan bulk UPDATE
Exclude soft-deleted accommodations from the UPDATE WHERE clause
Add @repo/logger call and { updated } return
Export from service-core barrel

tasks.title: Wire syncFeaturedByPlan to billing lifecycle events
tasks.description:
Hook the sync service into the billing lifecycle so that featuredByPlan is set/cleared automatically when the FEATURED_LISTING entitlement activates or lapses. Identify the billing event dispatch points in apps/api/src/ — look at subscription activation webhooks, cancellation handlers, past-due dunning outcome, and expiry cron paths (e.g. packages/service-core/src/services/billing/, apps/api/src/cron/jobs/, billing webhook handlers). Pattern: after any state transition that changes hasEntitlement(FEATURED_LISTING) for an owner, call syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, newActive).

The entitlement key is packages/billing/src/types/entitlement.types.ts:13. Plans that grant it: owner-basico, owner-premium, complex-pro, complex-premium (packages/billing/src/config/plans.config.ts). NOT complex-basico.

Key sites to hook (verify against current code):
- Subscription activation (webhook subscription_authorized_payment or status change to active)
- Subscription cancellation / soft-cancel after currentPeriodEnd
- Subscription expiry / past-due that crosses into entitlement loss
- Plan downgrade to a plan that does not grant FEATURED_LISTING

Key files (to read and modify):
- apps/api/src/routes/billing/ (webhook handlers)
- apps/api/src/cron/jobs/ (dunning, finalize-cancelled-subs, trial-expiry)
- packages/service-core/src/services/billing/subscription/ (status change helpers)

Acceptance criteria:
- Activating a FEATURED_LISTING-bearing plan triggers syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, true).
- Cancellation/expiry/past-due lapse triggers syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, false).
- No call on billing events for plans that do not grant FEATURED_LISTING (e.g. tourist plans, complex-basico).
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Integration tests in T-007 cover the activate + lapse transitions via mocked billing state.

subtasks.title: Identify all billing state-change sites that affect FEATURED_LISTING
Hook syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, true) on activation paths
Hook syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, false) on cancellation/expiry/lapse paths
Guard: only call for plans that grant FEATURED_LISTING

tasks.title: Implement featuredByPlan reconciliation cron
tasks.description:
Create a reconciliation cron that periodically audits featuredByPlan against the source-of-truth entitlement, correcting any drift that billing event hooks may have missed. Pattern mirrors apps/api/src/cron/jobs/trial-expiry.ts and other billing crons.

Create apps/api/src/cron/jobs/featured-by-plan-reconcile.ts:
- Fetch all distinct ownerId values from accommodations (non-deleted)
- For each ownerId, resolve their billing customer and call loadEntitlements + hasEntitlement(FEATURED_LISTING) (via the runtime gate at apps/api/src/middlewares/entitlement.ts:411/1014 or the underlying service)
- Compare resolved active state against current featuredByPlan on their accommodations
- Call syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, active) only when there is drift (avoid unnecessary writes)
- Log corrected owner count and total updated rows via @repo/logger
- Register the cron in the scheduler with an appropriate interval (suggested: every 6 hours, consistent with the cron-lag grace period documented in CLAUDE.md)

Key files:
- apps/api/src/cron/jobs/featured-by-plan-reconcile.ts (new)
- apps/api/src/cron/index.ts or cron scheduler file (register the new cron)

Acceptance criteria:
- Cron runs without error when all featuredByPlan values are already correct (zero-drift fast path).
- Cron corrects drift: an owner whose FEATURED_LISTING lapsed but still has featuredByPlan=true is cleared.
- Cron corrects drift: an owner who has FEATURED_LISTING active but featuredByPlan=false is set.
- Logs include corrected-count and total-updated for observability.
- pnpm typecheck green.

Testing requirements:
- Unit test: zero-drift path (no writes), drift-clear path, drift-set path. Covered by T-007.

subtasks.title: Create featured-by-plan-reconcile.ts cron job file
Implement per-owner entitlement check + drift detection + selective sync
Register cron in scheduler with 6h interval

tasks.title: Tests — admin regression, owner leak, sync service, search sort
tasks.description:
Four test scenarios covering all acceptance criteria of this spec. Run scoped (never the full suite) per the resource-care rule.

1. Admin toggle regression (G-1 confirmed): test that admin PATCH /api/v1/admin/accommodations/:id with { isFeatured: true } still persists isFeatured=true. Uses AccommodationPatchInputSchema. Assert the DB row reflects the change. This is a regression guard — no new code was written for the admin path, but this confirms it stays working.

2. Owner leak closure: test that owner PATCH /api/v1/protected/accommodations/:id with { isFeatured: true } does NOT persist isFeatured. After the patch, read the accommodation and assert isFeatured is unchanged (still false). The field should be stripped by the trimmed AccommodationUpdateHttpSchema.

3. Sync service unit tests (packages/service-core/...): mock the Drizzle db, call syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, true) — assert UPDATE with featuredByPlan=true WHERE ownerId AND deletedAt IS NULL. Call syncFeaturedByPlan(ownerId, false) — assert featuredByPlan=false. Empty-owner case: assert { updated: 0 }. Soft-deleted exclusion: rows with deletedAt set must not be updated.

4. Search sort integration: seed two accommodations — one with isFeatured=true, one with featuredByPlan=true, one with both false. Call the public list route (or buildAccommodationOrderBy directly) and assert the featured ones appear before the non-featured one. Also test that a listing with only featuredByPlan=true (isFeatured=false) still appears before a listing with both false.

Key files:
- packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/featured-by-plan.sync.test.ts (new)
- apps/api/test/routes/accommodation/ (admin regression + owner leak)
- packages/db/test/ or apps/api/test/ (search sort integration)

Acceptance criteria:
- All four scenarios pass.
- Admin toggle regression: PASS.
- Owner leak: isFeatured NOT persisted via protected PATCH.
- Sync service: set/clear/empty/soft-deleted-exclusion all correct.
- Search sort: featuredByPlan=true prepends the listing correctly.
- pnpm typecheck + lint green on test files.

Testing requirements:
- This IS the test task — run via: pnpm vitest run packages/service-core/test/services/accommodation/ and pnpm vitest run apps/api/test/routes/accommodation/ (scoped, not full suite).

subtasks.title: Admin toggle regression test (admin PATCH isFeatured persists)
Owner leak regression test (protected PATCH isFeatured not persisted)
syncFeaturedByPlan unit tests (set/clear/empty/soft-deleted)
Search sort integration test (featuredByPlan=true prepends)

tasks.title: Add SPEC-282 follow-up cross-reference note
tasks.description:
The spec §8 notes that the Listing destacado — Proximamente row in the SPEC-282 plan-comparison table should be flipped to active once this spec ships. That flip is tracked in SPEC-282, not built here. This task is the signal: once T-007 is green and the PR is merged, leave a cross-reference note for the SPEC-282 owner.

Add a brief comment in the SPEC-292 progress.md (or this state.json archiveNote field at close-out) pointing to the SPEC-282 plan-comparison table and noting the Listing destacado row can now show as active. No code change is required — this is a post-ship handoff note only.

Alternatively, if the featured listing row in the plan-comparison page is a simple i18n label change inside apps/web, and it is trivially doable (one-line change), include it here. Otherwise, defer to SPEC-282.

Acceptance criteria:
- A clear cross-reference exists in the task artifacts linking this spec to the SPEC-282 Listing destacado row follow-up.
- If the flip is trivial (one-line i18n or config change), it is done here; otherwise a note is left.

Testing requirements:
- None beyond a visual check of the plan-comparison page if the flip is done here.
